Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 040 [Gilgameš Letter][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na TI [...] ḪA LUGAL x-ra-nun-naki qi₂-bi-ma

(o 1) Say to ... [...] ..., king of ...-ranuna:

o 22

um-ma [...] ⸢LUGAL⸣ ŠEŠ.UNUGki DUMU KUL.ABA₄ki-ma

(o 2) Thus says [...], king of Ur, son of Kullab, creation of [...] and Ea, favourite of Šamaš, loved one of Marduk, who, like a [...] string, rules over all the lands from horizon to zenith and whose feet [...] (on) thrones kiss; [... who], like a string, is made to look over all the lands [...] until sunset, your lord.

o 33

bi-nu-ut [...] u de₂-a mi-gir dša₂-maš na-ram dAMAR.UTU

o 44

ša₂ ki-ma ⸢qe₂⸣-[e ...] ⸢ul⸣-tu AN.UR₂ a-di AN.PA KUR.KUR NIGIN-ši-⸢na⸣ i-be-lu

o 55

⸢u₃⸣ [...] BARA₂-MEŠ u₂-na-aš₂-ša₂-qu GIR₂-MIN-MEŠ-šu₂

o 66

[... ša₂ ... a]-⸢di⸣ e-reb dUTUši KUR.KUR NIGIN-ši-na GIM qe₂-e ⸢uš⸣-ta-bar-u EN-kam₂-ma

o 77

[... a-na qa]-⸢be₂⸣-e dBAD na-aš-pan-⸢ti⸣-im-ma

(o 7) [... at] the command of Ellil-of-Devastation:

o 88

[...]-⸢E₂⸣-ma lu₂EREN₂-MEŠ SIG-⸢MEŠ 6 ME-TA.AM₃ aš₂-pu-rarak-kam₂⸣-ma ul-te-bi-la

(o 8) [...] ... and I sent you 6 hundred prominent troops and had (them) brought.

o 99

[...] ⸢GAL⸣-MEŠ ša₂ na₄ZU₂ na₄ZA.GIN₃ KU₃.SI₂₂ liq-ti ⸢uḫ⸣-ḫu-zu

(o 9) I wrote to you [...] the great [...] of obsidian (and) lapis lazuli plated with liqtu-gold, to attach [...] chest of Enkidu, [...], and you said: "There are none".

o 1010

[... i]-⸢rat⸣ d⸢en-ki-du₃⸣ [...] ⸢ša₂ ra⸣-ka-si aš₂-pu-ra-kam₂-ma ia-⸢aʾ⸣-nu taq-bi

o 1111

[e-nen]-⸢na?⸣ al-tap-⸢rak-ka⸣ u₄-mu ṭup-pi šu-a-tu ⸢ta⸣-mu-⸢ru⸣

(o 11) Now I am writing to you (again). The day you see this tablet of mine, set out to go to the land of Eriš and take a team of horses with you!

o 1212

[ti-be]-⸢e⸣-ma a-na kure-⸢riš⸣ a-⸢lik⸣-ma KI-ka il₄-lat ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠe ⸢šu⸣-pur-ma

o 1313

[...] ⸢UR⸣.GI₇ tak-ri-ṣi ša₂ ki-ma UR.MAḪ da-an ti-bu-šu₂-un

(o 13) [n] gnawing away dogs whose attack is as strong as a lion's;

o 1414

[...] ⸢ANŠE⸣.KUR.RA-MEŠ BABBAR-⸢MEŠ⸣ ša₂ mi-kir-šu₂-nu ṣal-mu

(o 14) [n] white horses whose girth-straps are black;

o 1515

[...] ⸢ANŠE.KUR.RA⸣-MEŠ GE₆-MEŠ ša₂ mi-kir-šu₂-nu pe-ṣu-u₂

(o 15) [n] black horses whose girth-straps are white;

o 1616

[... munusANŠE.KUR].⸢RA-MEŠ ša₂⸣ KUŠ-ši-na pa-an ka-nak-ti KURi šak-⸢na⸣-at

(o 16) [n] mares whose skin is formed (like) the surface of a mountainous incense-bearing kanaktu-tree;

o 1717

[...] ⸢ša₂⸣ da-ka-ka la i-kil-⸢lu⸣-u₂

(o 17) [...] whose gamboling doesn't stop;

o 1818

[...] ⸢lim⸣ [...] pa-re-e bit-⸢ru⸣-mu-ti

(o 18) [n] thousand [...] of piebald mules;

o 1919

⸢50⸣ lim [... tak]-⸢di⸣-ri ša₂ ṣu-pu-ru bu-un-nu-u ⸢qar⸣-nu šal-⸢mu⸣

(o 19) 50 thousand spirited [...] whose hooves are beautified, whose horns are intact;

o 2020

⸢20⸣ lim [... I₃].⸢NUN⸣.NA 1.20 lim dugku-ut-tu gišGEŠTIN-MEŠ

(o 20) 20 thousand [...], [...] of butter, 80 thousand jugs of wine;

o 2121

1.20 lim [...] ⸢SAG.DU gišBANŠUR⸣-MEŠe GAL-MEŠ ša₂ ṣu-lum gišta-⸢ti⸣-[di]

(o 21) 80 thousand [...], [...] great tabletops of the black wood of a tatīdu-tree;

o 2222

1 me lim [...] a-na IGI-ka ⸢te⸣-ram-ma al-⸢ka⸣

(o 22) [...] 1 hundred thousand [...], gather (all this) before you and come.

o 2323

1 na₄ši-bir-⸢ti⸣ [...] ⸢lim⸣ tak₃-⸢kas₃⸣ na₄aš-pu-u ⸢na₄ZA⸣.GIN₃ NA₄-MEŠ KUR ma-la GAL₂ tak-ṣi-ri ina ⸢muḫ⸣-[ḫi ...]

(o 23) [...] 1 lump of [...]; on (it) [...] a string of amulet stones (with) [n] thousand small beads of chalcedony, lapis lazuli (and) mountain-stones, as many as there are.

o 2424

⸢40⸣ lim [...] ⸢a-na⸣ ERIM₄ EN GALe d⸢AMAR⸣.[UTU]

(o 24) 40 thousand [...] for the treasury of the great lord, Marduk.

Reverse
r 1r 1

1.30 lim ⸢GUN⸣ [...] ⸢na⸣-as₂-qu lat-⸢ku⸣ [be₂?]-⸢e⸣-ru aq-⸢ru⸣ [...]

(r 1) 90 thousand talents of [...] precious, tested, selected, rare [...], hammered, ... [...] - with it the smith will make stags.

r 22

maḫ-ṣu BE [...] ⸢KI?-šu⸣ lu₂⸢SIMUG?⸣ ia-a-⸢la i⸣-ip?-[pu-šu₂]

r 33

⸢1 me⸣ 20 lim GUN [...] lu₂SIMUG ši-pir ⸢E₂⸣.KUR ina lib₃-bi ip-pu-⸢šu₂⸣

(r 3) 1 hundred 20 thousand talents [...] - the smith will do work for the temple with it.

r 44

[a?]-⸢ra?⸣-nu eš-šu₂ ⸢ša?⸣ [... ru]-⸢u₂⸣-qu ša₂ ul-tu ma-⸢ti⸣-e IGI-MIN-MEŠ-a-a la e-mu-ra-ma SUMa u ri-qa

(r 4) A new coffin of [...] from afar, which my eyes have never seen, given or empty.

r 55

[...] ⸢lim? EREN₂⸣-MEŠ [...] ⸢a⸣-mur-ma ⸢a⸣-na a-ḫa-meš qur-rib

(r 5) Select [n] thousand troops [...] and bring (them) together!

r 66

[giš]MA₂.[GUR₈?-MEŠ ... mul-la]-am-ma ⸢it⸣-ti ⸢KU₃.BABBARim⸣-ma ⸢KU₃.SI₂₂⸣ a-na id₂BURANUN!(UD.NUN)ki ne₂-qel-pa-a

(r 6) Fill [...] boats [...] and float with the silver and gold down to/on the Euphrates!

r 77

[... DINGIR-MEŠ]-a-a ⸢li-mu⸣-ra-ma lib₃-⸢bi⸣ li-iḫ-mu

(r 7) [...] so that my [gods] may see (it) and my heart may be stunned.

r 88

[... ŠEŠ].⸢UNUG⸣ki la at-ma-⸢ru⸣-ka

(r 8) [...] I have not seen you [...] Ur,

r 99

[... ta]-mit-su-nu la ⸢up⸣-ta-sa-as-su

(r 9) [...] their oath cannot be annulled,

r 1010

[...] dUTU dIGI.DU dLUGAL.IR₉.RA dMES.LAM.TA.⸢E₃⸣.A

(r 10) [...] Šamaš, Palil, Lugalira, Meslamtaea,

r 1111

[...] ZIbu ša₂ ⸢MU⸣-šu tal-⸢ta⸣-nam₃-⸢ma⸣-[ru?]

(r 11) [...] ready [...], whose name you continually praise,

r 1212

[... E₂].⸢GAL⸣-MEŠ-ka i-maš-ša₂-aʾ ⸢giš⸣KIRI₆-MEŠ-ka x-x-⸢ma⸣

(r 12) [...] he will pillage your palaces, [...] your gardens,

r 1313

[...] x-e i-da-ba-⸢bu šu⸣-u₂-na-⸢ti?-šu₂⸣ e-rab-am-ma ⸢dGIŠ⸣-gim₂-maš

(r 13) [...] he speaks ... (and) I enter his dreams, so that Gilgameš

r 1414

[...]-⸢ši⸣-na a-a ip-qid₂-a-ni ⸢ia⸣-a-ši

(r 14) [...] he shall not entrust their (fem.) [...] to me.

r 1515

[...] ⸢ka-a⸣-ša₂ x [...] x x x [...]-ka DUMU-⸢MEŠ⸣-ka bu-ša₂-⸢ka⸣ u ⸢li⸣-da-ni-ka

(r 15) [...] you, ... [...], your ..., your sons, your property and your offspring

r 1616

[...] ⸢ŠEŠ⸣.UNUGki E₂? x x-ka u₃ ⸢ka⸣-ša₂ a-na E₂ lu₂⸢ZI? LUGAL? ŠEŠ.UNUG⸣ u₂-še-rab-ka-ma

(r 16) [...] Ur. He will make your ... and you enter the house of the ... of the king of Ur and

r 1717

[...] ⸢GUN?⸣-TA.AM₃ ⸢URUDU?⸣ x ⸢u₃⸣ 12 x x a-⸢šap⸣-[pa-rak-ka] U BU x A RA x KA? x a-šaṭ-ṭar

(r 17) [...] I will send you [...] talents each of copper ... and 12 ...; I will write ....

r 1818

[...] ⸢ŠEŠ⸣.UNUGki it-ti še-⸢de⸣-e la-mas-se-e ina ⸢KA₂?.GAL⸣-AM₃ u₂-ša₂-za!(A-)⸢az⸣-ka  ; [...] ⸢ŠEŠ⸣.UNUG⸢ki⸣ ina ⸢BAL⸣-ka ⸢ug⸣-da-ša₂-ru

(r 18) [...] Ur. I will make you stand with the protective (and) tutelary deities at each of the city gates; [...] Ur will dominate when they oppose you.

r 1919

⸢ar-ḫiš⸣ me-[ḫer ...] šu-⸢bi-lam-ma al⸣-kakam₂-ma ul ta-na-aš₂-ša₂-an-ni

(r 19) Quickly send me an answer [...] and come here; or will you not bring (it) to me?

r 2020

⸢INIM?⸣ d⸢GIŠ⸣-[gim₂-maš] ⸢LUGAL⸣ dan-nu ⸢ša?⸣ GABA.RI ⸢la⸣ i-šu-u₂

(r 20) Word of Gilgameš, strong king, who has no rival.

(colophon)
r 2121

LIBIR.RA.BI.GIM ⸢ša₃⸣-[ṭir ... mdIŠKUR]-⸢mu⸣-ša-mer šam-lu₃-⸢u₂⸣ šu-bul-⸢tin⸣-bi ⸢li-gi⸣-mu-u ⸢md⸣NERGALₓ(U.GUR)—tu-kul-ti ṭup-šar₄

(r 21) Written like the original [... Adad]-mušammer, the young scribal apprentice (and) descendant of Nergal-tukulti, scribe.

r 2222

ša IR d60 [...] ⸢IGI⸣-MIN-MEŠ-šu₂ ⸢IR⸣ ša ⸢ina URU₄? u₂⸣-šam-[šu-u ina] ŠU-MIN dMUATI ḫa-an-[ṭiš ...]

(r 22) Whoever takes away (this tablet), may Ea [...] take away his eyes! Whoever deliberately lets it be forgotten, [...] quickly by the hand of Nabu [...].
